steps.title: Translate the budget into an equation
steps.content:
The total expense budget $x$ equals the sum of departmental expenses ($y$) and all other expenses ($201$):
$$x = y + 201$$

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A: $x + y = 201$ would mean the two parts sum to 201 with no total, incorrect.
  • B: $x - y = 201$ matches our derived equation.
  • C: $2x - y = 201$ introduces an extra $x$ that has no basis in the context.
  • D: $y - x = 201$ implies departmental expenses exceed the total by 201, which is impossible.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
